title: Development and validation of a multi-locus DNA metabarcoding method
author: Alfred J. Arulandhu
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.jwzcpf6

steps.title: Perform the DNA isolation for all...
steps.instruction: Perform the DNA isolation for all the 11 samples simultaneously, including the DNA isolation of the negative control.

steps.title: Weigh 100 +/-10 mg per sample...
steps.instruction: Weigh 100 +/-10 mg per sample in a 2 ml tube. undetermined, Sample

steps.title: Add 300 μl nuclease-free molecular grade water
steps.instruction: Add 300 μl nuclease-free molecular grade water. undetermined, Nuclease-free molecular grade water

steps.title: Add 700 μl CTAB extraction buffer...
steps.instruction: Add 700 μl CTAB extraction buffer and vortex at maximum speed (~1000 rpm) for 10 seconds. undetermined, CTAB extraction buffer 00:00:10, Vortex

steps.title: Add 5 μl RNase (100 mg/μl)...
steps.instruction: Add 5 μl RNase (100 mg/μl) solution and gently shake the tube. undetermined, RNase (100 mg/μl) solution

steps.title: Incubate at 65 °C for 15...
steps.instruction: Incubate at 65 °C for 15 minutes in order to achieve efficient cell disruption in a Thermo-Shaker at maximum speed (~1000 rpm). 00:15:00, Incubation

steps.title: Add 20 μl of proteinase K...
steps.instruction: Add 20 μl of proteinase K solution (20 mg/ml) and shake the vial again. undetermined, Proteinase K solution

steps.title: Subsequently incubate at 65 °C for...
steps.instruction: Subsequently incubate at 65 °C for 1 h, Thermo-Shaker (1000 rpm). 01:00:00, Incubation

steps.title: Centrifuge the sample at ~18,000 x g for 10 minutes
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the sample at ~18,000 x g for 10 minutes. 00:10:00, Centrifugation

steps.title: Fill a 1
steps.instruction: Fill a 1.5 ml Eppendorf tube with 500 μl of chloroform undetermined, Chloroform

steps.title: Transfer the supernatant to the previously prepared 1
steps.instruction: Transfer the supernatant to the previously prepared 1.5 ml Eppendorf tube containing 500 μl of chloroform and shake the vial by hand for 30 seconds. 00:00:30, Shaking

steps.title: Centrifuge at ~18,000 x g for 10 min
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at ~18,000 x g for 10 min. The two phases are clearly separated. 00:10:00, Centrifugation

steps.title: Transfer upper layer to a new 1
steps.instruction: Transfer upper layer to a new 1.5 ml tube (~700 μl) and add the equal volume of (700 μl) chloroform and shake for 30 seconds. 00:00:30, Shaking undetermined, Chloroform The supernatant to be transferred shall be transparent, and any elements of the blurry interface will result in unsuccessful DNA extraction and/or strong PCR inhibition later on.

steps.title: Centrifuge for 10 minutes at ~18,000 x g
steps.instruction: Centrifuge for 10 minutes at ~18,000 x g. 00:10:00, Centrifugation

steps.title: Transfer the upper layer to a new 2 ml tube
steps.instruction: Transfer the upper layer to a new 2 ml tube. Based on previous experience ~500-600 μl (depending upon the sample) of upper layer can be transferred to the new tube.

steps.title: Add 2 volumes of CTAB precipitation...
steps.instruction: Add 2 volumes of CTAB precipitation buffer and mix by pipetting up and down. To keep it even add 1200 μl of CTAB precipitation buffer for all the samples.

steps.title: Incubate at room temperature for 1 h
steps.instruction: Incubate at room temperature for 1 h. 01:00:00, Incubation

steps.title: Centrifuge the sample for 10 min...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the sample for 10 min at ~18,000 x g and remove the supernatant, 00:10:00, Centrifugation

steps.title: Add 350 μl of NaCl (1
steps.instruction: Add 350 μl of NaCl (1.2 M) to the precipitate DNA (pellet not visible sometime). undetermined, NaCl

steps.title: Add 350 μl of chloroform to...
steps.instruction: Add 350 μl of chloroform to the tube containing precipitated DNA and NaCl, and vortex for 30 seconds. undetermined, Chloroform 00:00:30, Vortex

steps.title: Centrifuge the sample for 10 min...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the sample for 10 min at ~18,000 x g 00:10:00, Centrifugation

steps.title: Transfer the upper layer to a new 1
steps.instruction: Transfer the upper layer to a new 1.5 ml tube.

steps.title: Add 2 volumes of ice-cold ethanol...
steps.instruction: Add 2 volumes of ice-cold ethanol (96%) mix the sample by gently inverting the tube several times. Here, 700 μl of ice-cold ethanol needs to be added, to keep it even for all the samples.

steps.title: The samples are now stored overnight at -20 °C
steps.instruction: The samples are now stored overnight at -20 °C.

steps.title: On another day Centrifuge for 30...
steps.instruction: On another day Centrifuge for 30 minutes at ~18,000 x g and remove the supernatant. 00:30:00, Centrifugation

steps.title: Add 500 μl of ethanol (70%) to wash the pellet
steps.instruction: Add 500 μl of ethanol (70%) to wash the pellet. undetermined, Ethanol (70%)

steps.title: Centrifuge for 5 minutes at ~18,000...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge for 5 minutes at ~18,000 x g and remove the supernatant by pipetting. 00:05:00, Centrifugation Make sure that the supernatant is completely removed and also make sure not to displace the pellet.

steps.title: Air-dry the pellet for 1 hr
steps.instruction: Air-dry the pellet for 1 hr. 01:00:00, Air-drying

steps.title: Add 100 μl of water and...
steps.instruction: Add 100 μl of water and let the pellet dissolve overnight at 4 °C. undetermined, water

steps.title: On another day Vortex the dissolved...
steps.instruction: On another day Vortex the dissolved DNA and short-spin (10 sec) using mini-centrifuge. 00:00:10, Centrifuge

steps.title: The concentration of dissolved DNA is...
steps.instruction: The concentration of dissolved DNA is measured using by spectrophotometry (e.g. NanoDrop ND1000) according to the manufacturer’s protocol. No DNA yield should be present in the DNA isolation negative control.

steps.title: 100 μl of DNA is required...
steps.instruction: 100 μl of DNA is required for the experiment, dilute the DNA to a final concentration of 10 ng/μl using nuclease-free molecular grade water.

steps.title: Label (sample name, date) the 1
steps.instruction: Label (sample name, date) the 1.5 ml tubes containing the purified DNA and store the DNA at 4 °C.

steps.title: Prepare for each DNA barcode marker...
steps.instruction: Prepare for each DNA barcode marker primer pair (see the table below for primer information) a PCR mixture (12 in total) in a 1.5 ml Eppendorf tube using the provided chemicals and volumes listed under “Volumes (μl) X 13)” (Table 1). Primer labelling information: %s %s Before making the 12 master mixes, thaw the primers, vortex and spin down using mini-centrifuge. For example, use the primers 16SF and 16SR for PCR amplification of the full-length 16S marker. a

steps.title: Vortex the PCR mixture for 10 sec
steps.instruction: Vortex the PCR mixture for 10 sec. Vortexing will cause the liquid to be trapped in the cap of the tube. Before proceeding to the next step, make sure that this liquid is returned to the rest of the mixture by a15-second spin-down in a mini-centrifuge.

steps.title: Aliquot 20 μl of PCR mixture...
steps.instruction: Aliquot 20 μl of PCR mixture to each well according to the scheme in Table 3. undetermined, PCR mixture

steps.title: Aliquot 5 μl of sample DNA...
steps.instruction: Aliquot 5 μl of sample DNA template (10 ng/μl) to each well according to the pipetting scheme in Table 3. Add 5 μl of the positive control DNA according to the setup in Table 3.

steps.title: Use the provided Bos taurus DNA...
steps.instruction: Use the provided Bos taurus DNA (10 ng/μl) as positive control DNA for the animal barcode markers. Use the provided Lactuca sativa DNA (10 ng/μl) as positive control DNA for the plant barcode markers. Add 5 μl of ddH2O instead of DNA as negative control according to the setup in Table 3.

steps.title: Place the PCR tube strips in...
steps.instruction: Place the PCR tube strips in a thermocycler block with plastic caps on top and close the lid of the thermocycler. The second prepared example plate 2 can be stored at 4°C until further use in case only one PCR machine is available.

steps.title: Start the PCR program with the...
steps.instruction: Start the PCR program with the following extended thermal profile: a

steps.title: Examine the PCR thermocycler to ensure...
steps.instruction: Examine the PCR thermocycler to ensure that the run has completed at the correct temperatures and that there is no error messages.

steps.title: S – Sample, PA- Positive control...
steps.instruction: S – Sample, PA- Positive control animal, PP- Positive control plant and NC- Negative control after the PCR run has been successfully completed, spin down all the PCR tube strips for 15 seconds using a mini-centrifuge to make sure that any liquid that might be trapped under the cap is returned to the rest of the cocktail.

steps.title: Gently remove the cap of the PCR tube strip
steps.instruction: Gently remove the cap of the PCR tube strip.

steps.title: Visualise 5 μl of the sample...
steps.instruction: Visualise 5 μl of the sample S1-S10, positive control and negative control reactions by 1% agarose gel electrophoresis using an appropriate staining method (e.g. ethidium bromide) or on a microchip electrophoresis system. The fragment of the amplicons in the samples and the positive control reaction is verified by comparison to a 100 bp DNA Ladder. The expected fragment sizes of the amplicons are specified in the table below. a

steps.title: An example gel image is included in Figure 1
steps.instruction: An example gel image is included in Figure 1. No DNA bands should be observed for the negative control reactions.

steps.title: The amplified products in the PCR...
steps.instruction: The amplified products in the PCR tubes can be stored at 4 °C before pooling.

steps.title: On another day Vortex all the...
steps.instruction: On another day Vortex all the PCR tubes for 5 sec and spin-down using a micro-centrifuge for 10 sec. 00:00:05, Vortex 00:00:10, Spin-down

steps.title: Transfer 8 μl of each PCR...
steps.instruction: Transfer 8 μl of each PCR reaction mixture of sample S1 to a single 1.5 ml Eppendorf tube (row A, from A1 to A12, of Plate 1). In this way, all different barcoding markers of sample S1 are pooled.

steps.title: Repeat this procedure for samples S2 to S10
steps.instruction: Repeat this procedure for samples S2 to S10.

steps.title: The pooled PCR products of samples...
steps.instruction: The pooled PCR products of samples S1 to S10 are purified using the QIAquick PCR purification kit according to the manufacturer’s protocol QIAGEN (Annex 3). Ethanol is already added to the PE buffer, ready to use.

steps.title: Columns are eluted with 30 μl...
steps.instruction: Columns are eluted with 30 μl of the Elution buffer provided by the manufacturer. undetermined, Elution buffer

steps.title: Quantify the pooled and purified PCR products by spectrophotometry (e
steps.instruction: Quantify the pooled and purified PCR products by spectrophotometry (e.g. Nanodrop ND1000) according to the manufacturer’s instructions. If the concentration of the pooled and purified PCR products is less than 15 ng/μl, then repeat the QIAquick PCR purification step for these samples using higher volumes (> 10 μl) of the PCR reaction mixtures.

steps.title: For each sample, prepare an aliquot of at least 12
steps.instruction: For each sample, prepare an aliquot of at least 12.5 μl and with a concentration of at least 15 ng/μl.

steps.title: Label (sample name, date, and laboratory-number)...
steps.instruction: Label (sample name, date, and laboratory-number) the purified PCR products and store the amplicons at 4 °C.
